abstract: A non-fatal result indicating the peer certificate is valid, or was ignored if verification is disabled.
overview:
## Discussion

The peer’s certificate chain is valid, or was ignored if certificate verification was disabled via [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SSLSetEnableCertVerify]. In response, you may decide to continue with the handshake (by calling [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SSLHandshake(_:)] again) or just close the connection.

# errSSLPeerAuthCompleted

**Global Variable**

A non-fatal result indicating the peer certificate is valid, or was ignored if verification is disabled.

## Declaration

```swift
var errSSLPeerAuthCompleted: OSStatus { get }
```

## Discussion

The peer’s certificate chain is valid, or was ignored if certificate verification was disabled via [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SSLSetEnableCertVerify]. In response, you may decide to continue with the handshake (by calling [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SSLHandshake(_:)] again) or just close the connection.
title: errSSLPeerAuthCompleted
